支那冇簽Rome Statute

Territorial jurisdiction

The territorial jurisdiction of the Court includes the territory, registered vessels, and registered aircraft of states which have either (1) become party to the Rome Statute or (2) accepted the Court's jurisdiction by filing a declaration with the Court.[83]

In situations that are referred to the Court by the United Nations Security Council, the territorial jurisdiction is defined by the Security Council, which may be more expansive than the Court's normal territorial jurisdiction.[84] For example, if the Security Council refers a situation that took place in the territory of a state that has both not become party to the Rome Statute and not lodged a declaration with the Court, the Court will still be able to prosecute crimes that occurred within that state.


China

The People's Republic of China has opposed the Court, on the basis that it goes against the sovereignty of nation states, that the principle of complementarity gives the Court the ability to judge a nation's court system, that war crimes jurisdiction covers internal as well as international conflicts, that the Court's jurisdiction covers peacetime crimes against humanity, that the inclusion of the crime of aggression weakens the role of the UN Security Council, and that the Prosecutor's right to initiate prosecutions may open the Court to political influence.[81]

2019年8月11日23:52 警方晚上先後在港鐵葵芳站及太古站施放催淚彈。公共醫療醫生協會會長馬仲儀關注，警方在港鐵站內施放催淚彈，射出的彈殼可為市民帶來很大的直接衝擊，有如在室內開槍殺人一樣，批評警方妄顧市民生命，行為魯莽，並不專業，予以譴責。馬仲儀又說，催淚氣體的物質無直接致命元素，但擔心如果警方使用過期催淚彈，釋出的氣體累積至極高濃度，市民吸入後，除了感到呼吸不暢順或皮膚不適之外，亦可能影響中樞神經，例如導致癲癇症等，提高致命風險。她亦指出，催淚氣體所含的化學物質會依附於通風系統，關注乘客可能受有氣體影響，重申不應在室內施放催淚氣體，港鐵應向警方追究。

RTHK Live News
Chairperson of Hong Kong Public Doctors Association: Firing tear gas inside MTR stations could be equivalent to killing by firearm

11 Aug 2019 23:52 The Police has fired tear gas inside Kwai Fong and Tai Koo MTR station tonight. Arisina Ma, Chairperson of the HKPDA expressed concern over the Hong Kong Police Force’s actions, saying that in the process of firing tear gas inside an MTR station, the canisters may directly hit civilians, which would be equivalent to opening fire on and killing someone in an indoor area. Ma condemned the Police for neglecting civilian safety, and for acting unprofessionally and recklessly. Ma continued to point out although tear gas does not contain lethal substances, the use of expired tear gas, if built up to an extremely high concentration, may lead to potentially lethal consequences. Not only may people have difficulty in breathing and develop skin irritation, their central nervous system may also be affected, leading to illnesses such as epilepsy. Ma also pointed out that chemicals in tear gas may remain lodged within the MTR station’s ventilation system and affect future passengers. Ma stressed that tear gas should not be used inside MTR stations and that the MTR Corporation should hold the Police accountable.

I would like to report that during the months of June, July and August of year 2019, frequent street protests by the local citizens took place in Hong Kong. The protests were initiated to object to the Extradition Bill as proposed by the Hong Kong Government.

During the demonstrations, the protesters were largely peaceful and did not cause harm to any people. In contrast, the Hong Kong Police used unreasonable and escalating violence to disperse and arrest the demonstrators, including physical attacks, tear gas and plastic bullets aimed at heads.

As identified and verified by media records, the Hong Kong Police used tear gas indoors in a crowded train station against manufacturer's instructions as well as fired pepper bullet shots in close range of one (1) metre. Lethal concentration of tear gas can lead to fatalities, especially against old people or people with weak health.

The Hong Kong government refused to form an investigation committee into the brutality by the police nor take measures to relieve public anger.

In this regard, I would like to seek your assistance to instigate investigation into the unlawful operation and exessive violence of the Hong Kong Police, including the use of fatal force in handling protests.

Thank you for your kind attention. I hope by your international impartial status as well as the connection with the UN, you can help us to stop the violence and offences being commited by the Hong Kong Police, and bring justice upon this city.

We wish to underline that the tear gas does not only affect the protesters, but also unrelated walk-bys, including foreign visitors, who happen to be caught in the middle of the demonstrations. I hope your international, impartial status and your connection with the United Nations would help innocent Hong Kong residents by stopping the violence and offences still being done by the Hong Kong Police, and hopefully bring true justice to us.
